one_liner: MongoDB Atlas provides a broad REST Admin API (plus a Data API and MCP server) for managing clusters and data, secured via OAuth2 service accounts or legacy Digest API keys, self-serve via a free M0 tier.

auth_methods.notes: Recommended: service accounts using client credentials flow, exchanged for short-lived Bearer tokens at https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token
Legacy method using public/private key pair with HTTP Digest authentication; recommended to pair with IP access lists and key rotation

self_serve_notes: Free-forever M0 shared cluster tier allows self-serve account creation and API key/service account generation without sales contact; full access to Atlas Admin API and Data API included.

api_surface.notes: Atlas Administration API v2 covers cluster/project/org management; separate Data API provides REST access to database CRUD operations; Atlas CLI and Go SDK also available.

evidence.supports: Describes OAuth2 service account client credentials flow and legacy API key Digest auth for Atlas Admin API
Details Atlas authentication guidance across UI, database, and API access including workforce vs workload identities
Confirms free-forever M0 tier with full API, Search, and Data API access enabling self-serve development
Atlas Admin API v2 documentation showing breadth of REST endpoints for cluster and project management

flags: MCP server exists officially (mongodb-mcp-server) requiring either connection string or Atlas API service account credentials
Atlas-specific admin tools in MCP require Atlas API credentials, not just DB connection string
Legacy API key auth uses HTTP Digest, a less common auth pattern for tooling integrations
